content: We can frame each individual autonomous exploration agent as a discrete 'failed trajectory' for a collective population of self-supervised agents, where the entire population improves its exploration strategy by distilling self-supervised knowledge from the unproductive exploratory paths of individual agents that failed to discover high-reward regions. This creates a emergent population-level curiosity-driven loop that turns individual agent failure into collective exploration capability.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
trigger_topic: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
